The scheduled date of commercial operation (SCOD), the date of commercial

Asset 3 26.3....

The Petitioner also prayed for the grant of interim relief under Regulation 13(3) of the
2019 Tariff Regulations.

The Commission vide order dated 5.12.2025 approved 70% of the AFC claimed
as an interim tariff for Asset-1 and Asset-4 under Regulation 10(3) of the 2019 Tariff
Regulations.

However, no interim tariff was approved for Asset-2 and Asset-3 as they
have not been put into commercial operation, due to the non-readiness of the
Interim order in Petition No.211/TT/2024 Page 3 of 51interconnected generation under the scope of NHPC, and their COD was claimed under
Regulation 5(2)...

Regulation 5 (2) of the 2019 Tariff Regulation provides as under:
“In case the transmission system or element thereof executed by a transmission licensee
is ready for commercial operation but the interconnected generating station or the
transmission system of other transmission licensee as per the a...

As we are not deliberating in detail in this interim order, the prayer for the grant of
COD under Regulation 5(2) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations shall be considered in the
final order.”
“10.

As the Petitioner has filed the application in pursuance of Regulation 9(1) of the
2019 Tariff Regulations for the grant of interim tariff for its new transmission project under
Regulation 10(3) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations, this interim tariff is being granted to the
Petitioner after carrying out...

Accordingly, the interim tariff @ 70% of the AFC claimed in Asset-
1 and Asset-4 in the instant Petition shall be allowed for the 2022-23 and 2023- 24 tariff
period in terms of Regulation 10(3) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations.

However, the
Commission is not inclined to allow an interim transmission tariff for Asset-2 and Asset-
3 as the assets have not been put into commercial operation due to delay in
commissioning of generation asset under the scope of NHPC, and the tariff has been
claimed under Regulation 5(2) of the 2...

Taking into consideration that the issue of final order in the matter
would take some more time, we decide to grant an interim tariff for Asset-2 and Asset-
3 under Regulation 10(3) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations.

Regulation 10(3) and 10(6) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations provide as follows:
Interim order in Petition No.211/TT/2024 Page 4 of 51“10.

As per Regulation 10(3) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations, the Commission may
grant an interim tariff, if the information furnished by the Petitioner is adequate for
carrying a prudence check of the claims made, after hearing the parties concerned as
provided in Regulation 10(6) of the 2019 Tariff Regu...

The Petitioner has claimed the COD of Asset-2 and Asset-3 as 2.4.2023
and 26.3.2024, respectively, under Regulation 5(2) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations due
to the non-readiness of the interconnected generating station under the scope of NHPC.

NHPC, in its affidavits dated 24.6.2025 and 15.9.2025, has contested the
Petitioner’s claim for approval of COD of Asset-2 and Asset-3 as 2.4.2023 and
26.3.2024, respectively, under Regulation 5(2) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations.

The letters issued under Regulation 5(2) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations are not
as per the start date of connectivity.

The Petitioner has not complied with Regulation 5(2) of the 2019 Tariff
Regulations on the following counts:
i.

Non-Compliance of Regulation 27(1)(c)(i) of the Indian Electricity Grid Code
(IEGC) Regulations, 2023, (2023 IEGC Regulations).

The unilateral declaration of
COD by the Petitioner contradicts the fourth proviso of Regulation 27(1)(c)(i) of the
2023 IEGC Regulations.

Thus, the fourth proviso to Regulation 27(1)(c)(i) of the 2023 IEGC
Regulations, unequivocally mandates that the COD of a transmission element or
system shall not take effect unless approved by the Commission, and such
approval must be founded upon a certificate issued by the CTUIL confirming the
su...
